AMZN.reasoning:
MACRO REGIME: Transitional, leaning risk-on but increasingly fragile. S&P 500 hit ATH of 7,173 on April 27 before pulling back. Fed held at 4.25-4.50% (third straight hold), Powell's final meeting — Warsh succession adds policy uncertainty. Iran/Strait of Hormuz remains the primary tail risk; core PCE at 2.7% keeps the Fed constrained. Critically, Amazon earnings are due THIS WEEK and are flagged in the macro regime as a primary risk-off trigger ('big-tech earnings miss on AI capex ROI'). This alone disqualifies a swing entry — binary event risk invalidates the setup math.

RELATIVE STRENGTH: Strongly outperforming. Daily ROC: +3.92% (5d), +4.29% (10d), +30.28% (21d). All EMAs aligned uptrend on both daily and hourly. Price is 17.5% above 50-SMA and 16.75% above 200-EMA. Z-score vs 50-SMA = 2.0 — two standard deviations extended. AMZN is a sector leader but has already made the bulk of its move. Wiki thesis confirms system has been net bullish across all prior runs but consistently warned against chasing extensions.

HOURLY DISTRIBUTION SIGNALS: Hourly RSI = 48.9 (neutral, not confirming daily strength). Hourly MACD histogram = -0.4805 (bearish). Hourly OBV trend = DOWN and diverging from price — this is an early distribution signal, suggesting institutional selling into strength. Hourly ADX: minus_di (27.61) > plus_di (18.37), volume bias 10d = DOWN. These hourly signals indicate the move is rolling over on shorter timeframes while daily still looks extended.

ASYMMETRIC R/R: Current price ~$263. Near resistance $264.38 (hourly, 13 tests). Next Fib extension 1.272 = $282.28 (+7.2% upside). Stop below hourly support $260.34 (volume-confirmed, 19 tests): 1.0% downside. If using daily support $253: 3.8% downside. Best-case R/R: $282/$253 stop = 7.2%/3.8% = 1.9:1 — below the 2:1 minimum bar. Earnings binary event compresses this further — a gap-down on miss could blow through all supports. R/R does NOT meet entry criteria.

CAPITAL PRESERVATION: Daily RSI 14 = 86.14, RSI 21 = 89.35 — extreme overbought. Mean reversion risk is elevated. 30.28% 21-day ROC means we are late in this sector rotation cycle, not early. Sector cycle timing discipline says pass on late-cycle entries. AMZN balance sheet is strong (no leverage concern), but extreme valuation extension and earnings binary are sufficient flags.

CONCLUSION: The macro tailwind (risk-on lean, AWS/AI monetization thesis intact) is real, but the entry conditions are not met: (1) overbought on daily RSI 86+, (2) hourly OBV divergence signals distribution, (3) earnings this week creates binary risk that invalidates swing setup math, (4) R/R below 2:1 threshold, (5) late in the move. Wait for post-earnings pullback and RSI reset toward 50-60 before establishing a swing long. A clean re-entry zone would be $240-$248 on earnings-driven weakness if the fundamental thesis is confirmed.
